name: Zara Larsson
description:
'''Zara Maria Larsson''' ({{IPA|sv|ˈsɑ̂ːra ˈlɑ̌ːʂɔn|lang|sv-Zara Larsson.ogg}}; born 16 December 1997) is a Swedish singer and songwriter. She first rose to prominence in 2008 after winning the [[Talang 2008|second season]] of the Swedish talent show competition ''{{lang|sv|[[Talang (Swedish TV show)|Talang]]}}''. Larsson signed with [[Ten Music Group]] in 2012, and released her debut extended play (EP), ''Introducing'', the following year. Its single "[[Uncover (song)|Uncover]]" charted at number one in Sweden, where it was certified six-times [[Music recording certification|platinum]], and in Norway. Her debut studio album, ''[[1 (Zara Larsson album)|1]]'' (2014), spawned the Swedish top-ten singles "[[Carry You Home (Zara Larsson song)|Carry You Home]]" and "[[Rooftop (song)|Rooftop]]".

Larsson later signed a three-year contract with [[Epic Records]] in the United States, through which she released her debut international album, ''[[So Good (Zara Larsson album)|So Good]]'' (2017). The record became her second number-one album in Sweden, while charting in the top-ten in seven other countries. Its lead single "[[Lush Life (Zara Larsson song)|Lush Life]]" charted in the top-five in 18 countries and became one of the best-selling songs of the 2010s in the United Kingdom. The album features the follow-up singles "[[Never Forget You (Zara Larsson and MNEK song)|Never Forget You]]" (with [[MNEK]]) and "[[Ain't My Fault]]", both of which reached number one in Sweden, as well as "[[I Would Like]]", which reached number two in the UK. Larsson was featured on [[Clean Bandit]]'s 2017 single "[[Symphony (Clean Bandit song)|Symphony]]", which topped the [[UK singles chart]], as well as the Swedish and Norwegian charts. Her third studio album, ''[[Poster Girl (album)|Poster Girl]]'' (2021), was led by the single "[[Ruin My Life]]", which reached the top ten in the UK and Sweden, and earned a platinum certification in the United States.

In 2022, Larsson left Ten Music Group and acquired her music catalogue. Her fourth studio album, ''[[Venus (Zara Larsson album)|Venus]]'', was released in 2024 under Epic Records and her own label, Sommer House. Its singles "[[Can't Tame Her]]" and "[[On My Love]]" (with [[David Guetta]]) achieved commercial success in Europe, reaching the top five in Sweden and the top thirty in the United Kingdom. Larsson subsequently made her acting debut in the Swedish [[Netflix]] drama ''[[A Part of You]]'' (2024). Her fifth studio album, ''[[Midnight Sun (Zara Larsson album)|Midnight Sun]]'' (2025), was released to widespread critical acclaim. Its [[Midnight Sun (Zara Larsson song)|title track]] became Larsson's highest charting solo entry on the US [[Billboard Hot 100|''Billboard'' Hot 100]], and earned her a [[Grammy Award]] nomination for [[Grammy Award for Best Dance Pop Recording|Best Dance Pop Recording]]. In 2025, she collaborated with British singer-songwriter and producer [[PinkPantheress]] on the track "[[Stateside (song)#Zara Larsson remix|Stateside + Zara Larsson]]", which reached number one on the [[Billboard Global 200|''Billboard'' Global 200]] and the top ten on the Hot 100. 

In Sweden, Larsson has achieved a total of five number one singles on the [[Sverigetopplistan]] chart, three number one albums, with her EP [[Honor the Light|''Honor the Light'']] (2023) also topping the charts. [[List of awards and nominations received by Zara Larsson|She is the recipient of]] two [[American Music Awards]], a [[Bambi Award]], four [[Grammis]] awards, four [[MTV Europe Music Awards]], and has been nominated for four [[Brit Awards]] and a [[Grammy Awards|Grammy Award]], respectively.
